Save £25 on bookings with student and NHS discounts
Students can also save on everything from city breaks to beach holidays, perfect for some post-exam season adventures. Get £25 off bookings of more than £500 simply by verifying your eligibility on the Student Beans website.
If you or one of your group works for the NHS, you can take advantage of £30 off with a Blue Light Card when booking online, too.
It pays to do your research, though, as some travel operators offer better discounts for NHS workers. For example, with TUI’s holiday for heroes deals, you’ll get £100 off when you spend more than £1,200. This can often be combined with other TUI discount codes.
Free child places on selected bookings
Depending on when you’re travelling and where, one child may be able to travel for free with every two full-paying adults on select easyJet package holidays. Qualifying resorts will be clearly marked when you search on the easyJet holidays website, and these include popular family-friendly destinations such as Spain, Greece, Turkey and the Canary Islands.
Flights, accommodation and hotel activities are included. If you include transfers or meals in an adult booking, your child will also get these at no extra cost.
Just remember, as free kids' places are limited, they tend to get snapped up rather quickly, especially during the school holidays. It’s worth booking early to avoid missing out.
easyJet’s best price guarantee
If you happen to find the same holiday cheaper, within 24 hours of booking, easyJet holidays will not only match the price but beat it, by taking an extra £5 off per person (excluding infants and free child places). The identical holiday will need to be ATOL protected and provided by a travel agent or holiday company with an ABTA membership, as well as comprising the same flights, accommodation (including board basis and room type), transfers and baggage.
If the holiday meets these criteria, you can fill out this price promise form.
Why we recommend easyJet holidays
Free 23kg baggage allowance
Depending on your trip, booking a package holiday with easyJet holidays comes with a number of benefits. For those heading to the sea and sand, all beach holidays include a 23kg checked bag per passenger, as well as transfers to and from the hotel, allowing you to sit back and relax as soon as you step aboard.
Low deposits and flexible payment plans
One thing that can put us off booking a holiday is if it needs to be paid in full at the time of booking. The same can be said for deposits that are a large percentage of the price. But, thankfully, easyJet holidays offer what it calls Ultimate Flexibility – its holiday payment plan.
As long as you pay off the final balance 28 days before your holiday, you can pay in as many, or as few, instalments as you’d like. In most cases, all you need to pay is an initial deposit of £60 per person to secure the booking, allowing you to pay it off in a way that works for you. It’s also worth noting that you won’t need to pay any interest.
Cancellation and refund policy
If your plans change or another easyJet holiday happens to catch your eye after you’ve booked, you can cancel for free up to 60 days before departure and receive your full deposit back in the form of easyJet holidays credit. For those who have paid any instalments, above and beyond your initial deposit, these will be refunded to your original payment method up to 28 days before you were set to travel.
Alternatively, you can pay a one-off fee to change your holiday online, as long as it’s at least 28 days before you jet off, which might be a better option for some.
easyJet holidays FAQs
How we find the latest easyJet holidays discount codes
We work closely with the easyJet holidays team to bring you the latest and most reliable deals, promo codes and seasonal offers. Every code featured on this page is hand-tested by our team of deals experts to make sure it works before it’s published. Iran missiles fired towards UK military bases in Cyprus, defence minister says
United Kingdom
Unknown Publisher
2026-03-01
09:03:47 GMT
18:03:47 KST

Iran missiles fired towards UK military bases in Cyprus, defence minister says
Defence secretary John Healey has revealed that UK assets in the Middle East are at risk from Iranian retaliation
- Bookmark
- CommentsGo to comments
Two missiles from Iran were fired in the direction of Cyprus, where the UK has military bases, the defence secretary has revealed.
John Healey said the government did not believe they were targeting the bases intentionally, but said “it shows how indiscriminate” the Iranian retaliation to Saturday’s US-Israel strikes was.
He also said that 300 UK military personnel were close to targets that were hit in Bahrain.
John Healey told Sky News's Sunday Morning With Trevor Phillips programme: "This is a really serious and deteriorating situation, (with) rising risks of increasing Iranian indiscriminate retaliatory attacks.
"Let me give you a couple of examples. Yesterday, we had 300 personnel on that Bahrain base that was attacked by Iranian missiles and drones, some of them within a few hundred yards of where they landed.
"We had two missiles fired in the direction of Cyprus. We don't believe they were targeted at Cyprus, but nevertheless, it's an example of how there is a very real and rising threat from a regime that is lashing out widely across the region, and that requires us to act."
The revelations came a day after Sir Keir Starmer said the UK had ordered “planes in the sky” over the Middle East on defensive operations to protect our allies.
Sir Keir has also spoken to the president of Cyprus Nikos Christodoulides about the crisis.
In a statement President Christodoulides said: Prime Minister Keir Starmer called regarding the ongoing regional developments. He confirmed clearly and unequivocally that Cyprus was not a target. We are maintaining direct communication. All relevant authorities are fully engaged and monitoring developments closely.”
Mr Healey also repeated the prime minister’s calls for Iran to end its missile strikes and “steps back from its increasing uncontrolled indiscriminate attacks in the region” and “gives up its weapons programs.”
It came as the US and Israel launched waves of missile strikes on Iran in preemptive actions, taking out military infrastructure and the country’s leadership, including the Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei.
Mr Healey added: “Few people will mourn the Ayatollah’s death.”
The defence secretary said he was now more concerned about the repercussions of the attacks on Iran in the wider region.
He noted the 20 terror plots on the UK’s streets organised by Iran, the death of tens of thousands of its own citizens and the supply of 50,000 drones to Russia to attack Ukraine.
“Make no mistake that this is a regime which harasses other countries and can never be allowed to have a nuclear weapon.”
Mr Healey declined to comment on whether the US-Israeli strikes on Iran were legal.
He told Sky News: "No one who has seen the way that Iran has menaced us and sponsored terror around the world, been a source of instability in the region, is developing a nuclear weapons program, can be under any doubt about the character of this regime, and the threat it poses."
Asked again whether the US action was legal, he said: "That is for the US to set out and explain. It's not for me, as defence secretary of the UK, I'm here to speak for the UK.
"I can speak for the very active participation that we have in coordinated defence across the region."
He noted that “everything the UK does is within international law”.
But Mr Healey also declined to say whether the UK may be drawn into joining the US and Israel in direct operations on Iran.
Describing UK operations, Mr Healey said: "When our UK planes fly from Qatar, they are protecting against any missiles or drones directed towards Qatar.
"When they fly from Cyprus, they're doing the same for Cyprus.
"But of course, when our planes are in the air and they see things, missiles or drones directed towards other countries, they'll take them down.
"So when I talk about Britain playing a role in reinforcing regional stability as part of co-ordinated regional defensive operations, that's what I mean."
Meanwhile, opposition parties continue to raise questions over the government’s approach to the crisis.
Tory shadow foreign secretary Dame Priti Patel questioned why the prime minister was not more "proactive" in the run-up to the US and Israeli strikes on Iran.
She said: "A lot of this has been about why has Keir Starmer not actually worked with our American allies to be much more proactive?
"On the basis that a lot of intelligence is shared between our two countries, the Americans would easily have shared - and rightly so - details of their plans.
"Why were some of our bases not used? And was the British Government asked about use of our military bases in the defensive strikes that took place?"
She also called on the government to expel the Iranian ambassador.
However, following his party’s victory in the Gorton and Denton by-election last week, Green Party leader Zack Polanski said he has seen "no evidence" the UK took the process of diplomacy and negotiation with Iran seriously before it was attacked.
Mr Polanski said: "The moment you've ruled out negotiation, you've accepted that you're going to war, and I don't think the British people want to see another war in that region.
"The question becomes: how do you make diplomacy and negotiation work?
"What I know is negotiation was happening, whether it was working or not is a legitimate question, but I don't think the answer can be, it's not working, so we're going to bomb and kill them, the answer has to be: how do we work harder to make it work, including third countries to use to de-escalate?
"I've seen no evidence that the UK was really taking that process seriously, because part of the start of that would be to call out Donald Trump and Israel. There is only one region in that area with a nuclear weapon, and that's Israel."
Join our commenting forum
Join thought-provoking conversations, follow other Independent readers and see their replies

Hotels.com discount codes for March 2026
Exclusive eight per cent off with our Hotels.com voucher code
- Bookmark
Whether you’re looking for a city break, a European beach getaway or a Thailand backpacking adventure, Hotels.com has plenty of options to get excited about. Our team of deals experts is pulling back the cover on how to stay in your dream hotel for less with the best ways to save money on your booking, alongside the latest Hotels.com discount codes that we’ve listed above.
Saving at Hotels.com
Up to 20 per cent off with a One Key membership
”One Key” is a free loyalty programme, available for UK and US customers, where you work your way up the tiers for more benefits. All members start with a blue tier membership, moving up to silver, gold and platinum, unlocking more perks with each level. Here’s a breakdown of what you get:
- Blue: Save 10 per cent or more on more than 100,000 hotels worldwide
- Silver: Get 15 per cent or more off member pricing for 10,000+ hotels, plus three per cent OneKeyCash (aka rewards points) towards future stays and hotel food or drink perks.
- Gold: Save 20 per cent or more on thousands of hotels, plus room upgrade and 4 per cent OneKeyCash and hotel food or drink perks.
- Platinum: All of the above, plus six per cent OneKeyCash and VIP customer support.
You earn ‘OneKeyCash’ reward points on eligible bookings, starting at two per cent, the reward amount increases with each tier listed above. These accumulated reward points can be used towards future hotel or car hire bookings.
As well as Hotels.com, the One Key membership can be used on partner sites Expedia and Vrbo, meaning more opportunities to build up your perks and discounts.
You will automatically build up your OneKeyCash pot in your account after completing eligible trips, and your balance won’t expire, as long as you make at least one eligible booking within 18 months.
Save 10 per cent with student and NHS discounts
Students can get 10 per cent off on Hotels.com bookings via Unidays or Student Beans. Similarly, NHS staff and healthcare workers can also take advantage of this discount with a Health Service Discounts account. These offers can be used on top of existing member pricing or site-wide promotions.
To redeem your unique code from any of these sites, simply register with a valid ID or log in to your account, copy your code and apply it at checkout.
Get up to 20 per cent off with last-minute deals
Hotels.com doesn’t have a dedicated sale page, however, you can expect flash sales and limited-time promotions during seasonal events and key sales periods, such as Black Friday, Boxing Day, January, Valentine’s Day and in the run-up to summer.
If you’re flexible on dates or enjoy a spontaneous getaway, you can save 20 per cent or more with last-minute hotel deals. There are plenty of reduced rates on offer, including weekend breaks in cities such as London and Paris from £200 per night. You can even book rooms on the same day, which are sometimes cheaper than normal, as its the hotel’s last chance to fill the room.
Group discounts at Hotels.com
Hotels.com offers up to 70 per cent off for group bookings of nine rooms or more. From corporate travel and wedding parties to travelling with friends or family, it’s well worth getting a direct quote from the customer service team, to see if this beats any other rates. In fact, Hotels.com guarantees to meet or beat the price of any other quotes for the same number of rooms at another hotel of comparable quality.
Helpful FAQs
Where can I redeem my Hotels.com discount code?
To reveal and copy the code, click on the "See code" button on your chosen voucher. Continue to the booking confirmation screen, where you’ll see an “Apply a coupon code” field under the hotel summary section. Paste in your code and apply to see your booking cost reduced.
How much can I save with a Hotels.com promo code?
You can almost always save 10 per cent or more on your booking by applying a Hotels.com promo code, which can usually be combined with sales and limited time promotions across the site.
Can I use Tesco Clubcard vouchers at Hotels.com?
Tesco Clubcard members can double their voucher value, starting from £5, and use it towards any Hotels.com booking. For example, £10 worth of Clubcard vouchers is converted to £20 in Hotels.com credit. To redeem, simply log in to your Tesco Clubcard account in the app or online, exchange the voucher for a Hotels.com code and then paste it into the coupon field at checkout. If you encounter any issues, please try booking via the dedicated Hotels.com link on the Tesco Clubcard page.
Does Hotels.com still do reward nights?
No, the one-night free after 10 stays promotion has been replaced by the new One Key membership benefits, detailed further up this page.
How can I contact Hotels.com customer service?
Should you have any queries about your booking, you can contact the customer care team on 0203 027 6612 or on +44 203 027 6612 from outside the UK. It's always worth checking out the help centre (accessible at the bottom of all pages) for supporting articles on many frequent queries.

The team at The Independent specialises in finding the best travel deals and discount codes. We focus on great offers for hotels, flights and package holidays, ensuring that we only recommend deals we would use ourselves.
Our deals specialists work closely with Hotels.com to secure exclusive discount codes you won’t find anywhere else. Legoland Windsor Resort discount codes for March 2026
Here are the latest Legoland Windsor promo codes and offers
- Bookmark
With plenty of Legoland fun and savings to be had, we’re here to help you make the most of your visit for less. The Independent’s deal hunters have tracked down the best Legoland discount codes and rounded up the best money-saving tips to help you save on tickets and hotel bookings at the popular family attraction.
Legoland saving tips for 2026
Up to 50 per cent off online tickets
There are plenty of ways to save on your Legoland tickets, even when no big site-wide promotions are running. Right now, you can get up to 50 per cent off saver tickets when you book in advance online, with prices starting from just £32.
That means you still get to experience the park's newest additions – drench towers and splash safari – plus fan favourites such as the minifigure speedway, flight of the sky lion, hydra’s challenge and the dragon at more than half price compared with buying tickets on the day. If you do the latter, tickets can cost about £68, so you’re making a chunky saving by planning ahead.
During term time, parents of pre-school-aged children can take advantage of a joint ticket discount. From just £29, your child essentially gets free entry. Tickets must be booked online at least one day in advance.
Legoland short break deals
If you fancy extending your stay during the school holidays, it can often end up more cost-effective than a day drip. Plus, you’ll get added extras and park tickets thrown in. Case in point, you can stay from just £55 per person and receive the following benefits:
- One night’s stay at your chosen Legoland Resort hotel, plus breakfast
- One day Theme Park entry and early park access
- Free gift for children 11 years and under
- Free parking
Legoland also has a newsletter discount for £20 off short breaks. Sign up to receive a Legoland voucher code directly to your inbox that can be applied to your booking and stacked with the benefits mentioned above.
Save 20 per cent when you extend your stay
Those visiting during term time can save 20 per cent on bookings of two nights or more at the Legoland resort hotel, Legoland castle hotel or Legoland woodland village. Rates start from £103 per person for a two-night stay with breakfast included.
Merlin pass holders can also enjoy 15 per cent off Legoland sleepovers, with discounted rates at the resort hotel, castle hotel or woodland village. It's a brilliant way to get the full Lego experience, with themed rooms, evening entertainment and early park access.
Legoland student discounts
You can take a day off from studying and make use of the Legoland student discount, too, with tickets from just £21 when you verify your status through Student Beans. That’s almost 70 per cent cheaper than the £68 on-the-door ticket fee.
Annual pass benefits
If your family just can’t get enough of Legoland, consider purchasing annual passes. This way, you simply pre-book your tickets online each time you want to visit and you'll be covered. An annual pass also comes with extras that include:
- 10 per cent off retail shops
- 20 per cent off food and drinks
- Free parking
- Discounted adventure golf
- Discounted hotel stays
The three-month Legoland play pass is a brilliant option if you're not ready to commit to a full annual membership. For £59, you’ll get unlimited access for three months, which is great value for money, costing just over the price of two adult day tickets, but with unlimited access.
Helpful booking information
Merlin Pass benefits
Merlin annual pass holders can enjoy free entry to Legoland, with passes starting from £99. Remember to check your pass tier, as pre-booking is required and exclusion dates may apply depending on the type of pass you have.
Other benefits include:
- 20 per cent off adventure golf
- Up to 20 per cent off shopping and dining
- Free parking
- 50 per cent off for up to five friends and family members.
There’s also 10 per cent off the reserve and ride mobile function, which enables you to queue virtually for up to 25 rides, without having to physically stand and wait.
Kids under 90cm go free
If you’re attending as a family, remember that children under 90cm get free entry. You’ll also find “kids go free” offers available at certain times of the year, where one child (up to 15 years old) can enter at no cost when accompanied by a paying adult. It’s also worth noting that children under three can stay and eat for free at any of the Legoland hotels.
Legoland FAQs
How we find the best Legoland deals
The Independent’s team of deal hunters are dedicated to finding the best deals, whether that discounts on big-ticket items (think iPads, TVs or mattresses) or indeed family excursions. As Legoland offers something for everyone, we’re always on the lookout for new ways to help you save at the kids’ theme park, whether it's tickets, packages or hotel stays. We track the prices of tickets year-round, so you know you’re getting the best price. As soon as a Legoland promo code becomes available, we’ll verify it and share it here.

Deadly clashes at US consulate in Karachi over Khamenei’s killing
Unknown
Unknown Publisher
2026-03-01
09:01:46 UTC
18:01:46 KST

At least nine people were killed and several others injured in clashes between police and charged protesters who managed to enter the US Consulate in Pakistan's commercial capital Karachi on Sunday to protest the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in a joint US-Israel attack, an official confirmed.
Police surgeon Dr. Summaiya Syed told Anadolu that six bodies with bullet wounds were brought to the Civil Hospital.
Police fired teargas and resorted to aerial firing as hundreds of protesters marched toward the US mission in the city's southern district.
Dozens managed to enter the consulate premises and smashed the windows of the reception, multiple videos shared on social media platforms showed.
Clashes between the two sides were continuing till the filing of this report.
According to Faisal Edhi, head of the Edhi Foundation, an ambulance and welfare service, nine people were killed and 30 were wounded.

Club Med discount codes for March 2026
Get up to 20 per cent off resorts, with Club Med promo codes
- Bookmark
From sun-soaked beaches to snowy mountains, Club Med offers luxury all-inclusive holidays in more than 80 destinations worldwide, including the Maldives, Mauritius and the French Alps. With flights, transfers and accommodation all included, it makes travelling stress-free with good value for money. To help you save even more, we’ve rounded up the best Club Med voucher codes in our list above, as well as deals and booking tips to save even more below.
How to save on your Club Med holiday
Join the Club Med loyalty scheme
Regardless of whether you’re shopping with a Club Med promo code or not, you can get additional benefits by becoming a Great member for free. You'll accumulate points when you book, which stack up until you reach a new status. Then, you'll be able to enjoy exclusive benefits, such as free stays and more. You can even build your points when shopping at the resort.
There are four different levels in the Great Members loyalty programme:
- Turquoise: You automatically join this level when booking and will have access to the referral programme, the easy arrival service, personalised offers and the My Club Med app.
- Silver: Once you hit 20,000 points, your silver level will give you additional free early access to your resort with lunch included, a gift pass for a range of services, and free premium wifi.
- Gold: Users with more than 50,000 points get even more benefits, including priority check-in, private sales and early access to the sales openings.
- Platinum: When you hit a whopping 95,000 points, you’ll be a platinum member and enjoy private transfers, room upgrades, and priority bookings at reservation-only restaurants.
Early bird and last-minute Club Med deals
Booking early is one of the easiest ways to save, with up to 15 per cent off Club Med holidays when you plan ahead. There are already 2026 winter sun deals available, starting from just £799 for getaways to the Seychelles, Marbella, the Dominican Republic and Mauritius. If you prefer the mountains to the beach, Club Med’s all-inclusive ski holidays start from £1,549, with top destinations including Valmorel in the French Alps, Charlevoix in Quebec and Kiroro Grand in Japan.
Club Med also offers up to 20 per cent discounts on last-minute Club Med deals, which are updated each week for a departure date up to three weeks after you book, and only on bookings with a minimum of three nights. There are ski holidays included in these deals, so if a trip to Serre Chevalier was on your bingo card for 2026, you’ll be pleased to hear you can currently save on a stay at a beautiful ski resort in the area.
Get £200 credit when you refer a friend
You can also use the Club Med refer-a-friend programme to get 2,500 loyalty points and a £200 voucher to use on your next booking. To submit a referral, log in to your Club Med account and head to the “my referral rewards” page, where you can email a code for them to use when making their booking.
Your £200 credit is added to your account or via email 44 days before your friend's departure and is valid on all destinations year-round, plus it can be combined with other offers and promotions. Your referred friend will also receive a £200 voucher towards their next holiday.
Children stay for free
Club Med’s free child places offer another way to save on your family trip. For holidays in the sun, children under six stay for free, while under-fours go free for ski holidays.
Even better, childcare and kids’ clubs are included in your stay, with activities and expert supervision for children aged between four months up to 17 years. Whether it’s baby club, mini club or teen socials, there’s something to keep every age entertained while parents get a well-earned break.
Club Med offers
Club Med’s offer section has all of its latest promotions and sales in one place. From winter sun and ski escapes to cultural breaks, the selection of destinations and resorts on offer is updated regularly with hundreds of pounds worth of savings per person. It’s also worth keeping an eye out for its flash sale events – recently the summer 2025 flash sale had discounts of up to £800 per booking.
The Club Med dream deals page is packed with luxury holidays around the world that have been specially selected for limited-time discounts. You’ll find indulgent escapes, including Club Med Kani in the Maldives, with seven-night all-inclusive stays starting from £1,259 per person or Magna Marbella in Spain from £993. Dream deals also include ski destinations such as Pragelato Sestriere. If you’re flexible on dates or destinations, it’s one of the easiest ways to grab a great-value Club Med getaway.
We also recommend signing up for the Club Med newsletter for the latest flash sales, voucher codes and limited-time deals delivered straight to your inbox. Just scroll to the bottom of the Club Med website’s homepage to register.
Club Med skiing destinations to consider in 2026
If you’re hoping to escape to the slopes this winter, Club Med is known for its range of snow holidays that combine comfortable accommodation, access to world-class pistes and hassle-free planning. Here are three standout resorts to consider.
Val Thorens Sensations
Set in one of the highest ski resorts in Europe, Val Thorens gives you direct access to more than 600km of ski and snowboarding tracks. Week-long all-inclusive packages, including flights, range from £2,050 to £2,950 per person depending on your departure date and choice of accommodation.
Rooms are modern and cosy, and you can enjoy an impressive range of après-ski activities, including spa sessions and wine-tasting. What’s more, you can lock in those all-inclusive prices, so everything from transfers to ski passes will be taken care of.
Les Arcs Panorama
Perched at 1,600m above sea level, Les Arcs Panorama offers stunning alpine views and access to the wider Paradiski ski area. Perfect for mixed-ability groups, it features children’s areas, ski schools and off-slope activities.
For winter 2026, seven-night all-inclusive packages, including flights from London, range from £2,400 to £3,300, with late-season weeks between £1,800 to £2,000, covering meals, lift passes, lessons and resort amenities.
Peisey-Vallandry
Perfect for families or those seeking a quieter escape, Peisey-Vallandry features gentle slopes and ski-in/ski-out convenience in the Paradiski area.
Seven-night all-inclusive packages typically range from £2,400 to £2,500 per person from UK airports. Beyond skiing, you can enjoy scenic walks, cosy mountain restaurants or some downtime by the pool and spa.
Helpful booking information
Club Med guarantees
When you book with Club Med, you’ll have peace of mind knowing that your holiday is ABTA and ATOL-protected. Thanks to the travel operator’s price guarantee, the price you've paid won’t change with the cost of food, drinks, entertainment and activities always included.
If you’re booking a ski holiday, you’ll be covered by the snow guarantee. This means that if more than 50 per cent of your booked ski domain is closed for two or more consecutive days, within five days of your departure, you’ll have the option to cancel or rearrange your holiday. Keep in mind that cancellation excludes the cost of flights, transfers and extras.
Travel guides and inspiration
If you’re struggling for inspiration, Club Med has some helpful guides that are full of ideas to help you choose the right resort. It has roundups on paradise hotspots such as Mauritius, the Maldives and the Dominican Republic, as well as Mediterranean favourites such as Marbella and Sicily. Families can browse child-friendly resorts, with advice on the best all-inclusive clubs with activities for kids and free childcare.
For winter escapes, there are detailed guides for group ski holidays in the French Alps or Japan, including options in Val Thorens, Les Arcs and Kiroro Grand. You can even discover the best resorts in the mountains and lakes, with walking trails and scenic escapes around the Italian Lakes, Swiss Alps and Quebec’s Charlevoix region.
Cancellation policy
Club Med’s cancellation policy has a flexible approach on selected rates and a best-rate guarantee when booking. Club Med assistance insurance provides all guests with coverage for emergency medical expenses during their stay. You can choose to upgrade to Serenity Insurance, which has trip cancellation in case of unforeseen circumstances.
Where can I apply my Club Med discount code?
Copy your code by clicking the "see code" button on your chosen deal above. You can redeem your code on the Club Med website in two ways: either in the summary section at the end of the checkout process, or by clicking "View details of your stay" at the bottom of the screen. This text is available at any time while adding your personal information and extras.

Easy arrival service

Save up to £444 on solar panel and home battery installations with this exclusive Octopus deal

- Bookmark
Octopus Energy has launched a new, time-limited offer for Independent readers looking to cut the cost of installing solar panels and a home battery.
From 1 March to 17 April 2026, readers can get five per cent off the final price of an Octopus solar and battery installation, provided they meet the offer criteria. That’s a saving of up to £444, based on five per cent off the energy brand’s most popular system which includes 10 panels and a 5kWh smart battery. It’s worth noting that the actual discount will depend on your chosen system and for a full cost breakdown, there’s a handy calculator tool on the Octopus Energy website.
The timing will appeal to homeowners looking to cut their reliance on peak-time electricity, particularly as longer days return. Solar panels generate electricity from daylight, even on overcast days. Adding a home battery as well lets you store your surplus energy to use later, rather than exporting it immediately or buying more from the grid in the evening.
Octopus says a solar and battery setup can significantly reduce what households pay for electricity, depending on system size, usage patterns and tariff choices. The company claims some households could save around 90 per cent on electricity bills in certain scenarios, and highlights how batteries can also unlock smarter, time-of-use tariff strategies.
How to claim this exclusive deal
To qualify for Octopus Energy’s five per cent discount, homeowners must:
- Order a solar and battery system and pay a £500 deposit during the offer period
- Quote code ‘INDYSOLAR5’ to their solar sales specialist before signing the final installation agreement
- Note the discount is applied to the final installation agreement, not the initial online estimate
The offer is available to residential customers in Great Britain, is non-transferable, and can’t be combined with other promotions.
Key terms and conditions
This Octopus discount won’t apply if a customer cancels an existing installation agreement and places a new order for the same (or a substantially similar) system just to access the offer. The discount also becomes invalid if an order is cancelled, withdrawn from, or refunded.
As with any installation, everything is subject to eligibility, availability and technical feasibility, and Octopus’ standard solar and battery installation terms apply.
You can read all of the terms and conditions on Octopus’ offer page. You can also use Octopus’ solar calculator to see how much you might potentially save on your energy bills by installing solar panels.

Should Parramatta be filled with offices or rentals? The high-stakes tussle for space
Unknown
Unknown Publisher
2026-03-01
09:00:00 UTC
18:00:00 KST

Should Parramatta be filled with offices or rentals? The high-stakes tussle for space
As Parramatta’s skyline continues to push upward, a tug of war has emerged between city planners and developers over the CBD: what should space in the city centre be used for? Will it be a global engine for jobs, or used for high-rise housing?
The answer depends on how you picture Parramatta’s future.
The City of Parramatta council, for instance, believes the city needs more office space. It’s new strategy for the city’s economy shows that preserving and growing employment floor space across Parramatta as the population grows, is a priority. But it also warns that the current housing affordability crisis is driving pressure to surrender employment lands for residential use.
“The last thing you need is to just have houses within the Parramatta CBD, as an example, and then having to trek to the city or to other areas to find the job,” Parramatta Lord Mayor Martin Zaiter said.
“If we are going to have increases in housing density and increases in residential density, that also needs to be matched and met by additional job opportunities.”
To protect its ambitious goal of creating 150,000 new jobs by 2050, the council report said that all existing employment lands need to be preserved through planning controls.
But the council has to play a long game – recently revealed data from the Property Council of Australia Office Market Report shows total office vacancy in Parramatta rose to 22.1 per cent, up from 20 per cent six months ago, meaning the city has the fifth-highest vacancy rate in Australia.
Currently, the office space that is available is limited to spots across various floors, meaning large tenants cannot access multiple levels above each other – preventing more big businesses from becoming tenants.
With developers struggling to turn a profit on apartments in western Sydney, they are turning to one economic model that is proving to be the most feasible: build-to-rent. Unlike building to sell, which relies on strong pre-sales to fund construction, build-to-rent focuses on long-term rental income.
“Given that there are high vacancies across the A and B grades [office] stocks, build-to-rent is a good way to ensure that the city doesn’t become economically mothballed for the next 30 years,” Ross Grove, the western Sydney regional director for the Property Council of Australia, said.
Just down from Parramatta Square is the 47-storey HOME Parramatta tower, a new build-to-rent development that opened in January. Christian Grahame, head of HOME, expects more build-to-rent developments to be built over time, saying he has been “pleasantly surprised” with the level of demand for residential property in Parramatta.
“We’ve been able to lease about 10 per cent of it in the first couple of weeks with little or no marketing activity, and predominantly from local people who are attracted to Parramatta … that’s the place that they want to live.”
The Sydney Morning Herald has a bureau in the heart of Parramatta. Email parramatta@smh.com.au with news tips.

Whether you’re hoping to jet off to a sun-drenched island or see every inch of a historic city, travel retailer On the Beach has thousands of options for you to choose from. We’ve got the latest on bargain city breaks, the cheapest day to fly and the latest On the Beach discount codes and offers to help you get the most out of your budget.
Best ways to save at On the Beach
The PriceWatch deal of the week
The “PriceWatch” tool monitors more than 25,000 holiday prices, bringing you some of the lowest prices amongst travel providers. Every week, it spotlights one specific deal and compares its price to a competitor, like Tui or easyJet holidays, to help you save up to £1,000.
Last-minute holidays
On the Beach deals change weekly, so make sure you keep checking back for news on the best discounts, as you never know what you’ll find. If you’re hoping for last-minute sunshine over the holidays, you’re in luck. On the Beach has a wide selection of holidays starting from less than £200 per person, from the Algarve to Dubai. How quickly can you pack that bag?
Book early and save on 2027 holidays
Looking ahead to 2027 means low prices and availability you won’t find on 2026 holidays. In fact, travel writer Amelia Neath has found the cheapest time for UK travellers to book trips. Domestic flights “can be more affordable when booked 170 to 180 days before travel, saving up to 36 per cent compared to leaving it around six to 12 days before,” she wrote. Typically, Saturday is the cheapest day to fly, up to 18 per cent cheaper than a Monday, which is the most expensive day of the week.
On the Beach has 2027 holiday deals to popular destinations like Gran Canaria, Egypt, Florida and the United Arab Emirates, with prices starting from £599 per person. You’ll find sunny favourites such as Benidorm and Majorca, along with Cape Verde and Egypt, and long-haul packages to the Maldives and Caribbean.
Holiday perks
Aside from weekly discount codes and deals, there are always other ways to save. Why book with On the Beach?
Low deposits
You can secure most holidays from £30 per person, which is one of the lowest booking deposits offered amongst travel providers. Last-minute bookings of less than 45 days before departure do not qualify for a low deposit option.
Price guarantee
Another hack to know is On the Beach's price guarantee, which means that if the price of your holiday drops at least 60 days before you depart, you can claim back the difference in the form of a voucher. You can check if your holiday is eligible through the On the Beach app where you’ll see exactly how much you can claim and how to get the credit straight to your account.
Cancellation policy
The On the Beach cancellation policy for holiday packages varies depending on the time before departure; however, it’s stricter than other travel companies like Tui and easyJet holidays. Here’s a breakdown of fees:
- 60 days or more before departure: a £50 fee per person, plus supplier charges.
- Less than 60 days before departure: £100 fee per person, plus supplier charges.
Supplier charges can be as much as 100 per cent of the total cost, meaning that cancellation charges vary by booking. Flights are also non-refundable from the point of booking, so it’s worth bearing this in mind before confirming your holiday.

Russian shelling in Kharkiv region leaves seven injured
The head of the Kharkiv Regional Military Administration, Oleh Syniehubov, reported this on Facebook, according to Ukrinform.
“Over the past day, the city of Kharkiv and 20 settlements in the Kharkiv region came under enemy fire. In the village of Rozsokhuvate of the Kunie community, two 20-year-old men and a 25-year-old man were injured; in the village of Oskil, a 66-year-old woman; in the village of Anyskine of the Vilkhuvatka community, a 51-year-old man. In the city of Kharkiv today, a 75-year-old woman and a 69-year-old woman were injured,” Syniehubov said.
According to him, as a result of the shelling in Kharkiv, a dormitory, shop windows, and a fence were damaged; in Bohodukhiv district, power lines and an outbuilding; in Kupiansk district, private houses, a hotel, and a cultural center; in Izium district, private houses, power lines, a school, and a car; in Chuhuiv district, a farm where animals were injured, a car, and a warehouse.
A transit evacuation center in Lozova received 98 people over the past day.
As reported by Ukrinform, in the Shevchenkivskyi district of Kharkiv, Russian forces attacked a dormitory with a drone, causing a fire.

Shrapnel falls in Beersheba, major road damaged near Beit Shemesh as IDF strikes central Tehran
Unknown
Unknown Publisher
2026-03-01
08:58:16 UTC
17:58:16 KST

Iran’s state television headquarters in Tehran was reportedly attacked Saturday, according to Sabereen News, a news agency affiliated with pro-Iranian militias in Iraq. Footage circulating on social media showed smoke rising over Tehran.
Israel’s Home Front Command said residents across the country, including in the south and central regions, could leave protected spaces but must remain nearby. Earlier instructions had required some areas to remain inside shelters following missile launches from Iran.
In the latest barrage, debris fell in Beersheba, damaging a vehicle but causing no injuries, according to Israel’s Magen David Adom emergency service. Additional damage was reported to a major road near Beit Shemesh.
Israel’s Security Cabinet is scheduled to convene at 9 p.m., officials said.
Meanwhile, IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir visited Hatzerim Air Base on Tuesday to review the Air Force’s readiness ahead of Operation Roaring Lion, the military said. Speaking to personnel, Zamir referenced a previous operation and said the Air Force had demonstrated “impressive capabilities,” highlighting close coordination with the United States, including US Central Command and US Air Forces Central.
“When we enter this campaign, extraordinary power will be brought to bear, ultimately achieving the required objectives,” he said, according to the IDF.
Air raid sirens sounded across large parts of Israel on Saturday morning after missile launches were detected from Iran, as the Israel Defense Forces confirmed it had killed Iran’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, in the opening strike of Operation Roaring Lion.
The IDF said it was now striking “regime terror targets in the heart of Tehran” for the first time since the operation began. According to the military, the Air Force, guided by military intelligence, launched a broad wave of strikes against regime targets in central Tehran.
The Home Front Command said alerts were activated nationwide after launches from Iran were identified and urged the public to follow safety instructions. The Air Force was operating to intercept incoming missiles and strike where necessary to remove threats.
Sirens were heard in Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, the coastal plain, the Sharon region, the West Bank, the Jordan Valley, the Galilee, the Golan Heights, Haifa and as far south as Beersheba, the western Negev and communities near the Gaza border. The Home Front Command later said residents across the country, including in the north, could leave protected spaces but should remain nearby.
The latest barrage was relatively limited, similar to previous rounds, with most projectiles intercepted and several falling in open areas. Israel’s Magen David Adom emergency service said it had received no immediate reports of injuries and dispatched teams to several locations.
A projectile landed in an open area near Beit Shemesh following one of the launches. Fire and Rescue Services reported that interceptor debris in the West Bank caused a gas leak in one community.
Earlier, Ichilov Hospital in Tel Aviv said a woman in her 60s who experienced shortness of breath while heading to a protected room during the sirens was pronounced dead despite resuscitation efforts.
The IDF also confirmed that the fatal strike in Tel Aviv a day earlier, which killed a live-in caregiver, was caused by a direct missile hit rather than interceptor fragments. Tel Aviv city officials said 40 buildings were damaged in that strike, one declared uninhabitable, and more than 200 residents were evacuated to three hotels.
Home Front Command chief Maj. Gen. Rafi Milo conducted situational assessments and visited impact sites. The command has mobilized about 20,000 reservists. Over the past 24 hours, its call center received roughly 65,000 phone calls and more than 9,000 digital inquiries.
In the region, Oman’s state news agency reported that the commercial port of Duqm was targeted by two drones. The US Embassy in Muscat advised its staff to shelter in place. Authorities in Bahrain said the Crowne Plaza hotel was hit by an Iranian missile. In Dubai, an official said two people were wounded by debris from intercepted drones over residential areas.
Pakistan’s media, cited by Al Jazeera, reported that protesters stormed the US Consulate in Karachi in response to Khamenei’s killing, with casualties reported.
Meanwhile, Sabereen News, affiliated with pro-Iranian militias in Iraq, reported that Ahmad Vahidi, a former Iranian interior minister, had been appointed the new commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.
Israel’s finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich, instructed that there be no budgetary cap on evacuating residents whose homes were rendered uninhabitable to hotels nationwide, though officials said only those whose homes are deemed unfit will qualify.
The Home Front Command said challenging days lie ahead and that strict defensive policies remain in effect as the conflict continues.

Chessington discount codes for March 2026

- Bookmark
If you need inspiration for a fun-filled family day out, there are several fantastic theme parks here in the UK, including Chessington World of Adventures. The park is home to more than 40 rides and attractions, along with a zoo and aquarium with more than 1,000 animals. Our team of deal hunters has been hard at work sourcing the best discounts and deals to help you save on Chessington tickets.
How to save on Chessington tickets
Up to 50 per cent off online bookings
By booking in advance, you’ll save up to 50 per cent on your Chessington tickets. Booking online slashes the price considerably on single-day tickets, which include entry to the theme park and the zoo. Online tickets start from £32, but if you were to buy walk-up tickets on the day, you’d pay upwards of £66.
If you’re planning to turn your trip to Chessington into a staycation, consider the great deals on overnight stays with park entry. These offers start from £32 per person, with entry to both the theme park and zoo, free breakfast, early ride access, free parking and hotel entertainment. You can choose from themed and standard rooms, which the kids will love (think Paw Patrol, Jumanji, Oreo, Capri-Sun, Gruffalo and Room on The Broom rooms). If you’ve got a little extra time to spare, Chessington’s overnight deals are a total steal.
Find tickets for £21 with the Chessington student discount
If you’re still in education, Chessington World of Adventures offers an exclusive student discount to Student Beans members. Enjoy the on-site theme park, zoo and Sea Life Centre at a reduced rate of just £21 – that’s 31 per cent cheaper than the standard online day ticket cost and 68 per cent cheaper than the on-the-gate price.
Simply sign in or register with Student Beans to redeem your Chessington promo code and prebook your ticket online. On the day of your visit, you’ll need to present a valid student ID alongside your booking confirmation.
Use your Tesco Clubcard points to save
Tesco Clubcard points offer a simple way to make a trip to Chessington World of Adventures a bit more affordable, especially if you’ve built up a healthy balance of points from your weekly shop. Instead of using your points in-store, you can exchange them for Clubcard rewards, which can be used towards the cost of entry tickets.
To get started, log in to your Tesco Clubcard account, head to the rewards section and check whether Chessington is currently available. If it is, you can convert your points into reward vouchers and apply them at checkout when booking your tickets online. As offers can change, make sure you check ahead and try to combine your Clubcard rewards with other Chessington discount codes and deals to stretch your budget even further.
It’s also worth keeping in mind that availability does change throughout the year and you may not be able to redeem your points during peak seasonal events.
Get 20 per cent off with the Merlin holiday club
If you want to save extra, join the Merlin holiday club and get up to 20 per cent off your short breaks. Available to all Merlin annual pass holders, the holiday club gets you discounts on overnight stays in themed hotels, including the on-site safari and Azteca resort hotels, and glamping options. Short breaks must be booked through the Merlin website for this reduced rate to be applied.
All hotel stays at Chessington include a wild-themed room, free buffet breakfast, free parking, evening hotel entertainment, access to the Savannah Splash Pool, free wifi and early access to the park between 9–10 am on selected dates.
Save more than 10 per cent with Blue Light Card tickets
Blue Light Card holders can take advantage of exclusive Chessington member days, offering theme park and zoo access for £25 per person (a regular pre-booked ticket costs around £29).
Tickets are available on a first-come, first-served basis by logging in to your Blue Light Card account, searching for “Chessington member day” and booking your tickets through the link provided.
The £25 ticket fee covers both adults and children (under-threes also need a ticket, but entry is free), and includes complimentary parking and access to the zoo area. Accommodation must be booked separately, and these special offers are non-transferable and non-refundable.
New Paw Patrol theme park to open at Chessington this spring
Chessington is getting ready to open the UK’s first World of Paw Patrol this spring, bringing Adventure Bay to life for children aged two to five. The 1.4-acre attraction will feature four themed rides, including a gentle rollercoaster, alongside interactive play areas, character meet-and-greets, a dedicated food outlet and a themed shop.
Children can also apply for the important new role of “pawject manager”, giving one lucky super-fan the chance to preview the attraction before it officially opens.
While the confirmed launch date is still to be announced, tickets for late spring and summer are already available to book online, with advance savings of up to 48 per cent compared with on-the-gate prices (though early spring visitors should note the land may not yet be open).
To turn the trip into a short break, Chessington has new Paw Patrol-themed hotel rooms ready to stay in, too. They sleep up to five guests for an immersive overnight stay with added perks such as early ride access and VIP experiences.

How many Chessington discount codes can I use at once?
Chessington only lets you redeem one discount code per booking. Coupons or codes cannot be combined, so it’s best to pick the code that gives you the biggest saving.

Is there a Chessington NHS discount?

You will get the biggest savings by booking in advance or choosing off-peak dates. You can also save up to £30 on annual pass offers. Seasonal promotions are worth keeping an eye on, too, especially around Halloween, school holidays and the summer.

Emirates discount codes for March 2026
Get up to 10 per cent off with the latest Emirates promo codes and student discounts
- Bookmark
Flying to popular destinations such as Bangkok, Sydney and Singapore, and with a reputation for in-flight luxury, it’s no surprise Emirates trips tend to come with a handsome price tag. So that you can enjoy the experience for less, our team of deal hunters have rounded up the best Emirates promo codes to help you save on your travel costs.
Emirates offers several ways to save, from joining its Skywards loyalty programme to using student discounts and taking advantage of special offers throughout the year. Whether you’re booking a dream getaway or business travel, there are plenty of ways to stretch your budget further. Here’s our roundup of the best ways to save at Emirates.
Our tips to save at Emirates
Emirates Skywards and Skywards+ member benefits
Skywards is the loyalty programme that Emirates shares with Flydubai and is designed to offer its members benefits and experiences that work with their lifestyle, and make holidays even more rewarding.
Join for free to begin collecting miles and other benefits, depending on the tier level. Here’s a breakdown:
- Blue (upon registration) – Instant upgrades (when available), wifi messaging in economy, and priority waitlists.
- Silver (25,000 tier miles) – all of the blue benefits, plus 30 per cent bonus miles, 12kg additional baggage and priority check-in.
- Gold (50,000 tier miles) – all of the silver benefits, plus 75 per cent bonus miles, free seat selection, extra baggage and selected worldwide business-class lounge access.
- Platinum (150,000 tier miles, plus one qualifying business- or first-class flight) – all of the gold benefits, plus 100 per cent bonus miles, access to the Emirates first-class lounge in Dubai and more
To enhance your benefits even further, join Skywards+, which gives subscribers the choice of three levels – classic, advanced and premium – all with their own advantages, to give you personalised deals.
Even with the lowest tier of membership, classic, you'll get a one-time 20 per cent discount on a classic reward flight, which could save you hundreds on that dream holiday to historic Oman or trendy Brisbane. The savings alone will pay for the annual subscription, and that’s without the other benefits – including bonus miles, airport lounge access, and extra luggage.
Families can also combine their miles in a My Family account, allowing up to eight members to contribute to a shared balance. Younger travellers can join Skysurfers – a dedicated loyalty programme for children aged 2–17.
Up to 10 per cent off with the Emirates student discount
Students aged between 16 and 31 can get in on the fun. Use the student code provided above for up to 10 per cent off economy and 5 per cent off business-class fares to more than 250 destinations worldwide. You’ll also get 10kg more baggage, or one extra piece, on top of the usual allowances, and flexible options to change your flight.
Please note, you must bring your valid student ID or school acceptance letter when you check in for your flight. Otherwise, you will no longer qualify for the promotion and will be required to pay what the fare costs on the day of departure.
Save 50 per cent with weekly special offers
Check out the Emirates special offers page for limited-time promotions on flights and to maximise your Skywards benefits. Previous deals have included bonus miles, upgrade offers and up to 50 per cent off when you pay with Skyward miles.
Emirates best fare finder
The “best fare finder” tool helps you get the lowest fares to more than 135 destinations worldwide. You can easily compare prices by month and view which days are cheapest to travel.
It’s faster than using other third-party flight comparison sites, plus Emirates offers a best price guarantee, which means they’ll refund the difference if you find the same flight for £10 or more cheaper elsewhere.
Book in advance and save up to 55 per cent
When it comes to travel, typically, the earlier you book, the better the deal you’ll find. In fact, our travel team recently explored this and found that “booking short-haul flights five months in advance can save you just under 50 per cent across flight and accommodation costs, whereas six months is recommended for long-haul with further price reductions rising to 55 per cent”.
You can often find lower fares and access early bird holiday offers, particularly for long-haul destinations. MyBenefits cardholders booking a flight and hotel together through Emirates Holidays can also save 5 per cent on packages.
Helpful FAQs
Where can I redeem my Emirates discount code?
You can apply your discount code when you search for your flights. Under the departure, arrival and passenger information, you will find a “use promotional code” section. Click there to reveal a box where you can enter your code.
Is there an NHS discount at Emirates?
Unfortunately, Emirates doesn't offer an NHS discount, however, you can still take advantage of the many other discounts. As soon as any NHS promotions become available, we'll update this page.
What is the baggage policy at Emirates?
Baggage allowances depend on your fare type and destination. Economy passengers usually receive 20-35kg, while business- and first-class customers get 40-50kg.
When can you check in for an Emirates flight?
You can check in for your Emirates flight online 48 hours before, up until 90 minutes before departure.
How we find the best Emirates deals
Consumer editor Sabrina Sahoto and The Independent’s team of deal hunters are dedicated to carefully selecting the best travel offers and discount codes. We look out for deals on flights, package holidays and in-flight shopping; we’d never recommend a deal that we wouldn’t use ourselves. It’s here where you’ll find the most up-to-date offers available at Emirates to help you save on your 2026/27anua holiday.
Why choose Emirates?
“I’ve been following the Emirates story since the very first flight almost 40 years ago. The growth of the Dubai-based airline has been astonishing. Emirates now serves more than 150 destinations on six continents and has the world’s biggest fleet of Airbus A380 ‘SuperJumbo’ aircraft. Many travellers appreciate the extra space they offer, as well as the unrivalled in-flight entertainment.
“Emirates also excels at connecting Britain with the Middle East, Asia, Africa and Australia, with 18 daily flights from six UK airports taking thousands of people each day to Dubai and beyond,” says travel correspondent Simon Calder.

Pakistan bombs Bagram air base in Afghanistan
Unknown
Unknown Publisher
2026-03-01
08:56:49 UTC
17:56:49 KST

ALBAWABA- Pakistan carried out airstrikes on Afghanistan’s Bagram Air Base late Friday, marking a significant escalation in rapidly deteriorating relations between the two neighbors.
Local sources in Parwan province said three Pakistani fighter jets targeted the sprawling airfield north of Kabul, once a major U.S. military hub, triggering multiple explosions. Sections of the base were reportedly damaged, including facilities allegedly used by Taliban forces for drone operations. Initial reports indicated at least four people were killed and 14 injured, though casualty figures have not been officially confirmed.
Taliban authorities imposed restrictions on media coverage of the strike sites, warning local outlets against reporting from affected areas.
The attack comes amid intensifying cross-border hostilities that have pushed the two countries into open confrontation. Last Thursday, Afghan Taliban forces claimed to have seized 17 Pakistani border posts and killed 40 Pakistani soldiers, describing the action as retaliation for Pakistani airstrikes days earlier in Nangarhar, Paktika and Khost provinces.
Islamabad said those earlier strikes targeted Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) hideouts and killed more than 70 militants, while Afghan officials claimed 18 civilians also died.
Pakistan followed with further bombardments in Kabul, Kandahar and border regions on February 27. Defense Minister Khawaja Asif declared that Islamabad had exhausted its patience over what it calls Kabul’s harboring of militants, signaling a shift toward “open war.”
Casualty figures from the week-long clashes remain sharply disputed. Pakistan says nearly 300 Taliban fighters have been killed and two of its own personnel lost. Afghan officials claim 55 Pakistani soldiers have died, 19 posts were destroyed and several troops captured.
Taliban spokesman Zabihullah Mujahid condemned the latest strikes as violations of Afghan sovereignty, while signaling openness to dialogue potentially mediated by Qatar or Turkey. Pakistani officials maintain their operations are aimed solely at countering terrorism threats inside their territory.
The renewed violence mirrors deadly clashes in 2025 that displaced thousands and strained a fragile ceasefire reached in October. The United Nations has called for immediate de-escalation, warning of mounting humanitarian risks for communities along the volatile border.

China calls killing of Iran’s supreme leader ‘unacceptable’
By
China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi sharply criticised the killing of Khamenei in a phone call with his Russian counterpart Sergei Lavrov.
“The blatant killing of the leader of a sovereign state and the incitement of regime change are unacceptable,” he was quoted as saying by China’s official Xinhua News Agency. “These actions violate international law and the basic norms governing international relations.”
He said attacking a sovereign state without UN Security Council authorisation undermines the foundation for peace established after World War II.
“The international community should speak in a clear and unequivocal voice, opposing the world regressing to the law of the jungle,” he said.

By Josefine Ganko
Singapore Airlines has confirmed that it has cancelled all flights to and from Dubai for one week, ending March 7.
The flights impacted are SQ494 departing Singapore for Dubai, and SQ495 departing Dubai for Singapore.
The fallout of the conflict for air travel has been widespread, with planes largely grounded in Dubai, Abu Dhabi and Doha, each major international travel hubs.
German airline Lufthansa has suspended flights to Tel Aviv, Beirut, Amman, Erbil, Dammam and Tehran until and including March 8, due to the current situation in the Middle East, it said on Sunday, extending cancellations already in place.

Advertisement
yesterday 11.46pm
At least nine killed as protesters try to storm US consulate in Pakistan
By
At least nine people were killed and about two dozen were wounded in violent clashes with police and paramilitary forces Sunday after hundreds of protesters tried to storm the US consulate in the Pakistani port city of Karachi, authorities said.
The violence came hours after the United States and Israel attacked Iran and killed the country’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Police and officials at a hospital in Karachi said at least 25 people were also wounded in the clashes and some of them were in critical condition.
Summaiya Syed Tariq, a police surgeon at the city’s main government hospital, confirmed that initially six bodies and several injured people were brought to the facility. However, she said the death toll rose to nine after three of the critically wounded died.
The US embassy in Pakistan wrote on X that it was monitoring reports of ongoing demonstrations at the US consulates general in Karachi and Lahore, as well as calls for additional protests at the US embassy in Islamabad and the consulate general in Peshawar. It advised US citizens in Pakistan to monitor local news, stay aware of their surroundings, avoid large crowds and keep their travel registration with the US government up to date.
Karachi is the capital of southern Sindh province and Pakistan’s largest city.
Senior police official Irfan Baloch said protesters briefly attacked the perimeter of the US consulate but were later dispersed. He dismissed as baseless reports that any part of the consulate building was set on fire. However, he said protesters torched a nearby police post and smashed windows of the consulate before security forces arrived and regained control.
Witnesses said dozens of Shiite protesters remained gathered about a kilometre from the consulate, urging others to join them. They said one of the protesters had tried to burn a window of the consulate before security forces arrived and dispersed the demonstrators.
It prompted Pakistan’s Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi to issue an appeal for calm.
In a statement, he said, “Following the martyrdom of Ayatollah Khamenei, every citizen of Pakistan shares in the grief of the people of Iran.” He described it as “a day of mourning for the Muslim Ummah and for the people of both Iran and Pakistan”, but urged people not to take the law into their own hands and to express their protests peacefully.
Shiites make up roughly 15 per cent of Pakistan’s population of about 250 million and represent one of the largest Shiite communities in the world. They have frequently staged anti-Israel and anti-US rallies in the past, though clashes of this scale are rare.
Reuters
yesterday 11.10pm
Australian man ‘under the missile flight path’ in Dubai
By Angus Dalton
Lee Caldwell moved from Brisbane to Saudi Arabia last week to start work with a new business and was in Dubai visiting friends when explosions began rattling the roof with debris from falling missiles yesterday.
“We seem to be right under the missile flight path as we have been seeing tons of intercepts right above us,” Caldwell, a drone engineer, said.
“The whole house shakes when [the missiles] hit then we hear the shrapnel on the roof. It sounds like someone throwing handfuls of gravel.”
Caldwell and his friends have been told to stay inside and avoid public places such as shopping malls. They’re stocking up on groceries and have no idea when they’ll be able to leave as about 10 missiles per hour soar over the neighbourhood.
“All we can do is stay inside … as shrapnel is a problem.
“We had just moved to Saudi Arabia for work and had decided to visit friends in Dubai for a weekend. Turns out it was safer in Saudi.”
Several people across Dubai have been injured by falling shrapnel.

By
Israel said it launched a broad wave of strikes in central Tehran on Sunday and was seeking to dominate the skies over the capital, after its air force killed Iran’s supreme leader in a large-scale assault that has raised fears of widening instability in the Middle East.
Over the past day, Israel’s air force conducted strikes to open the “path to Tehran”, and the Israeli military said the majority of aerial defence systems in western and central Iran had been dismantled.
Israeli military spokesperson Lieutenant Colonel Nadav Shoshani told reporters that many targets remained, including military-industrial production sites. “We have the capabilities and the targets to keep going on for as long as necessary,” he said.
Asked whether Israel was considering deploying ground forces to Iran, Shoshani said that was not under consideration even though US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu have urged Iranians to seize a rare opportunity to topple their leaders.

At least 6 killed as Shiites storm US Consulate in Pakistan over killing of Iran’s supreme leader
Unknown
Unknown Publisher
2026-03-01
08:53:02 UTC
17:53:02 KST

9 killed as protesters try to storm US Consulate in Pakistan over killing of Iran’s supreme leader
9 killed as protesters try to storm US Consulate in Pakistan over killing of Iran’s supreme leader
KARACHI, Pakistan (AP) — Violent clashes between protesters and security forces in the Pakistani port city of Karachi left at least nine people killed and more than 50 others wounded on Sunday, after hundreds of demonstrators attempted to storm the U.S. Consulate, authorities said.
Live from Karachi as hundreds storm the U.S. consulate in Pakistan’s port city after the killing of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
The violence came hours after the United States and Israel attacked Iran and killed the country’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Police and officials at a hospital in Karachi said that at least 25 people were also wounded in the clashes and some of them were in critical condition.
Summaiya Syed Tariq, a police surgeon at the city’s main government hospital, confirmed that initially six bodies and multiple injured people were brought to the facility. However, she said the death toll rose to nine after three critically wounded people died.
US monitoring protests
The U.S. Embassy in Pakistan wrote on X that it was monitoring reports of ongoing demonstrations at the U.S. Consulates General in Karachi and Lahore, as well as calls for additional protests at the U.S. Embassy in Islamabad and the Consulate General in Peshawar. It advised U.S. citizens in Pakistan to monitor local news, stay aware of their surroundings, avoid large crowds and keep their travel registration with the U.S. government up to date.
Karachi is the capital of southern Sindh province, and Pakistan’s largest city.
Senior police official Irfan Baloch said that protesters briefly attacked the perimeter of the U.S. Consulate, but were later dispersed. He dismissed as baseless reports that any part of the consulate building was set on fire. However, he said that protesters torched a nearby police post and smashed windows of the consulate before security forces arrived and regained control.
Witnesses said that dozens of Shiite protesters remained gathered about a kilometer (half-mile) from the consulate, urging others to join them. They said one of the protesters had tried to burn a window of the consulate, before security forces arrived there and dispersed the demonstrators.
Appeal for calm
The clashes prompted Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi to issue an appeal for calm.
“Following the martyrdom of Ayatollah Khamenei, every citizen of Pakistan shares in the grief of the people of Iran,” Naqvi said in a statement.
He described it as “a day of mourning for the Muslim Ummah and for the people of both Iran and Pakistan,” but urged people not to take the law into their own hands and to express their protests peacefully.
The provincial government of Sindh in a statement also urged citizens to express their views peacefully and warned against engaging in violence.
Protests in the area surrounding the U.S. Consulate in Karachi went on for hours, with dozens of Shiite youth, some covering their faces, throwing stones at law enforcement officials and vowing to reach the consulate, where hundreds of police and paramilitary Rangers have been deployed.
Unrest expands
In Islamabad, police fired tear gas and swung batons as hundreds of Shiite protesters, angered by the killing of Khamenei, tried to march toward the U.S. Embassy. The clashes took place outside the Diplomatic Enclave, where the embassy is located and additional police had been deployed.
Meanwhile, in the northwestern city of Peshawar, authorities also used tear gas and batons to disperse thousands of demonstrators attempting to approach the U.S. Consulate to hold a rally to denounce the killing of the Iranian leader, police said.
Shiites also held a peaceful rally in Multan, a city in Punjab province, chanting slogans against Israel and the United States. Mamoona Sherazi, who attended the rally, said that she was protesting Khamenei’s killing. She described him as a fatherly figure and a strong voice for Shiites, adding that he also supported Sunni Muslims facing oppression. “God willing, we will never bow before America and Israel,” she said.
Shiites also held a rally and clashes with police repeatedly near the U.S. Consulate in Lahore, the capital of eastern Punjab province, police said. Rallies against Israel and the United States were also planned in northern Gilgit-Baltistan region. Authorities said that the government has stepped up security around the U.S. Embassy in the capital, and consulates across the country to avoid any further violence.
Shiites make up roughly 15% of Pakistan’s population of about 250 million and represent one of the largest Shiite communities in the world. They have frequently staged anti-Israel and anti-U.S. rallies in the past, though clashes of this scale are rare.
___
Munir Ahmed reported from Islamabad. Babar Dogar in Lahore, and Asim Tanveer in Multan, contributed to this story.
